name: A11y Expert MCP
description: MCP server that gives AI coding assistants accessibility expertise. Reviews code, suggests fixes, provides WAI-ARIA patterns.
features: 4 MCP tools: review_code, get_pattern, list_patterns, check_contrast
33 WAI-ARIA component patterns in knowledge base
WCAG contrast ratio checker with AA/AAA pass/fail
Code review with specific fix suggestions and ARIA references
Published on GitHub as open-source
MCP resources: pattern index + per-component detail (a11y://patterns)
5 guided prompts: audit-component, make-accessible, check-form, wcag-checklist, aria-guide
Prompts auto-inject relevant WAI-ARIA patterns as context

tasks.task: Publish to PyPI
tasks.description: Package and publish the A11y Expert MCP server to PyPI for easy install.

tasks.task: Add component library research tool
tasks.description: Tool to research and compare accessible component libraries.

tasks.task: Remove check-form-accessibility prompt (audit-component with component_type=form does the same thing)
tasks.description: Cleanup: remove redundant prompt.

name: A11y App
description: Android accessibility app — helps users customize UI for their needs. High contrast, color schemes, AI-driven simplification.
features: Research phase — competitive analysis of 10+ Play Store apps
Feature matrix comparing existing accessibility tools
Product plan with MVP scope defined
MCP product plan for AI-powered accessibility

tasks.task: Fix high contrast mode — highlight card borders & non-text UI elements
Add color scheme options — dark mode, light mode, custom user color picker
Implement AI-driven UI customization (simple language, layout switching, chart captions, text simplification)

name: Hermes Dashboard
description: Bento-grid dashboard — tasks, memory, cron, skills, projects. Elli's single source of truth.
features: Bento-grid home with 12+ cards (tasks, memory, cron, skills, projects, etc.)
A11y widget with 10 features (contrast, fonts, motion, reading guide, etc.)
Rendered markdown in memory/user/soul pages with clickable file paths
Project tracker with task tables, difficulty and impact ratings
Auto-refreshing data.js from JSON sources
GitHub Pages deployment with git push

tasks.task: Add mobile breakpoints to all detail pages
Add main landmarks, aria-hidden on icons, focus-visible styles
Security hardening — CSP, pin Lucide + SRI, Object.create(null)
A11y panel — custom font picker, color pickers for text/background/links
Consolidate 3 health check crons into 1

name: Hermes Agent
description: Improve Hermes and specialize it for Elli's workflow: less noise, better project state, clearer coding loops.
features: Active project to improve Hermes for Elli-specific workflows
Separate work from status updates to reduce context waste
Keep project state in small files like PLAN.md, TODO.md, and DECISIONS.md
Use checkpoint-only updates: done, blocker, next, need input
Reduce narration and progress spam during coding tasks
Apply these ideas project by project instead of changing everything at once
Dashboard now split into user-facing Config & Setup and separate Dashboard Internals with a visual flow map

tasks.task: Separate work updates from status updates in responses
Add PLAN.md / TODO.md / DECISIONS.md pattern for active coding projects
Use checkpoint-only updates (done, blocker, next, need input)
Reduce verbose progress narration during coding tasks
Create a full visual agent map / repo map for Hermes (big-picture system overview)

name: Dashboard Rebuild
description: Rebuild the Hermes dashboard from scratch using React + a vetted accessible design system. Goal: component-based architecture so all pages share the same templates, header, layout — no more 24 separate HTML files.

tasks.task: Research phase: Research accessible design systems: Shopify Polaris, Radix UI, Adobe Spectrum, Carbon — compare a11y compliance, theming, React support
Research phase: Research Storybook — evaluate if useful for this project scope
Research phase: Decide: React + Vite (static, GitHub Pages) vs Next.js (needs Vercel/server)
Research phase: Define teal/sea color palette as design tokens to map onto chosen system
Research phase: Document current pages inventory: list all pages, their data sources, and component needs
Architecture & setup: Create separate dev branch or repo (e.g. hermes-dashboard-v2) — keep v1 in production
Architecture & setup: Set up React + Vite project with chosen design system
Architecture & setup: Define shared component library: PageLayout, Header, Card, StatusBadge, CodeBlock, Table
Architecture & setup: Define routing structure (React Router or file-based)
Architecture & setup: Set up data layer: how data.js feeds React components (context, props, or store)
Build core components: Build Header component (logo, status dots, restart button) — shared across all pages
Build core components: Build PageLayout component (header + page title + body + footer)
Build core components: Build Card component matching current bento grid cards
Build core components: Build RenderedMarkdown component (replaces renderMd() util)
Build core components: Build CodeBlock component (replaces raw-box)
Migrate pages: Migrate index.html (home/bento grid)
Migrate pages: Migrate tasks.html
Migrate pages: Migrate projects.html
Migrate pages: Migrate cron.html
Migrate pages: Migrate all remaining pages (skills, memory, sessions, etc.)
QA & launch: Accessibility audit on rebuilt dashboard (axe, keyboard nav, contrast)
QA & launch: Mobile responsiveness check on all pages
QA & launch: Deploy v2 to GitHub Pages (separate URL or replace v1)
QA & launch: Decommission v1 once v2 is stable

name: A11y Engineering Toolkit
description: Modular accessibility engineering platform for developers, QA, and product designers. Modules are independently installable. Built for learning and demonstration — showing how to improve product accessibility through testing, visual auditing, and UX customization. Starting with Hermes dashboard, then Melio.

tasks.task: Research code annotations as a11y constraints — JSDoc tags, ESLint rules, CSS comments
Add pa11y-ci alongside axe in GitHub Actions — runs HTMLCS + axe together, catches more issues
tasks.description: Research finding: pa11y-ci runs both axe and HTMLCS engines. Catches things axe misses. Free and open source.

tasks.task: Evaluate IBM Equal Access Checker as a third CI layer — 400+ rules, free, open source
tasks.description: Research finding: IBM Equal Access has broader rule coverage than axe. Worth evaluating as an audit-level check.

tasks.task: Build AI-augmented PR suggestions — axe finds violation, LLM reads HTML context, suggests fix as PR comment
tasks.description: Research finding: nobody has shipped this in production. Real gap. Architecture: axe violation → LLM reads surrounding HTML → suggests accessible name/fix → posts as PR comment. Estimated 2-3 day build.

tasks.task: Define axe rule profiles: developer profile (skip contrast) and design-review profile (contrast + color only)
tasks.description: Use axe tags (wcag2a, wcag2aa, best-practice) to create separate profiles. Developers shouldn't be blocked by contrast issues — that's a design concern.

tasks.task: Define toolkit module list and install interface (npm, MCP, GitHub Action, CLI)
Set up GitHub repo for a11y-engineering-toolkit

Research Claude Code skills format, then build two focused skills for the toolkit: one for designers (visual, contrast, color, typography) and one for developers (keyboard, ARIA, screen reader, semantic HTML). Add to .claude/commands/ in toolkit repo.
Module: GitHub Actions workflow — run axe-core on every push, fail on WCAG AA violations
tasks.description: Start with Hermes dashboard GitHub Pages URL as test target. Reusable workflow so Melio can adopt it.

tasks.task: Module: GitHub Actions — add PR comment with a11y violation report
Module: MCP server — integrate existing a11y-expert-mcp as toolkit module
Module: CLI tool — run a11y audit from terminal on any URL
Module: AI agent prompts — reusable agent workflows for a11y review, ARIA fix, contrast check
Write README with module installation docs
Test full pipeline on Hermes dashboard — prove it works end to end
Adapt pipeline for Melio — document what changes for a React/enterprise codebase
Module: A11y Audit Panel — extract a11y-tester.js from dashboard into a standalone embeddable script (drop-in for any webpage)
tasks.description: Currently lives in the Hermes dashboard as a11y-tester.js. Package it so QA and developers can drop it into any site to run visual accessibility audits without installing anything.

tasks.task: Module: A11y UX Widget — extract a11y-widget.js from dashboard into a standalone embeddable script (contrast, fonts, motion, reading guide, color picker)
tasks.description: Currently lives in the Hermes dashboard as a11y-widget.js. Package it so product designers can demo live accessibility UX customization on any site.

tasks.task: Write educational guide: how to use the toolkit to demo a11y improvements to product designers and QA
tasks.description: Target audience: designers and QA at Melio. Show how each module works and what problems it solves visually.

tasks.task: Create demo page showing all toolkit modules in action
tasks.description: A single page that demos: audit panel, UX widget, CI results, MCP usage. For showing to Melio colleagues.

tasks.task: Research Generative UI — understand how it works, existing implementations, accessibility angle
tasks.description: Starting point: https://research.google/blog/generative-ui-a-rich-custom-visual-interactive-user-experience-for-any-prompt/ — Research saved to vault A11y App Research folder. Key questions: can it run on any site? Vercel AI SDK streamUI? Accessibility use case (user-directed UI regeneration).

tasks.task: Module: Generative UI — let users say 'show this differently' and regenerate the layout using AI
tasks.description: AI-driven UI customization: user describes their preference and the interface adapts. Explore: Vercel AI SDK for own sites, browser extension approach for any site. Strong a11y use case — users with cognitive/visual needs can request simpler layouts, larger spacing, plain language.

features: A11y Audit Panel (a11y-tester.js) — visual in-page accessibility auditor with issue highlighting
A11y UX Widget (a11y-widget.js) — floating customization panel: contrast, fonts, motion, reading guide, color blindness simulation

name: Melio Conversational UI (MVP)
description: Voice/chat interface on top of Melio's app — lets users ask in plain language: what's outstanding, pay this, add a bank account. Accessibility play: removes the need to navigate complex tables. No internal repo used — built on top of public-facing Melio API calls captured via DevTools.

tasks.task: Research: Find and install Chrome DevTools MCP — enables agent to inspect network calls, DOM, console directly from browser
tasks.description: Candidate: AgentDeskAI/browser-tools-mcp or similar. Search npm/GitHub, install, configure in Hermes config.yaml.

tasks.task: Research: Check if Melio has a public API or developer docs
tasks.description: Check melio.com/developers, app.melio.com network tab, and any public API docs. No internal repo.

tasks.task: Research: Map Melio's key API endpoints via DevTools network tab
tasks.description: Log into Melio, open DevTools Network tab, perform actions (view bills, initiate payment, add account), capture endpoint shapes. Document in vault.

tasks.task: Research: Check Melio's ToS for automated access / scripting restrictions
tasks.description: Quick read of melio.com/terms — flag anything that blocks local MVP use.

tasks.task: Research: Check if Melio already has a conversational/chat feature — and what it can do
tasks.description: Is it a support bot or action-taking? What gaps does the MVP fill?

tasks.task: Build: MCP agent layer — tools wrapping Melio API calls (get_outstanding, pay_bill, add_bank_account, add_card)
tasks.description: Python MCP server. Tools call Melio endpoints with user's session token. Use Claude Opus via delegate_task.

tasks.task: Build: Simple chat UI — text input, plain language responses
tasks.description: Next.js or plain HTML+JS. Connects to MCP agent. Runs locally first.

tasks.task: Build: Add voice input — Web Speech API (browser STT, no extra cost)
tasks.description: Press-to-talk button, transcript fed to the same chat handler. Works in Chrome.

tasks.task: Demo: Record a Loom of the full flow — ask, pay, add account
tasks.description: Show it running locally. Use real Melio account. Prepare 2-min demo script.

tasks.task: Demo: Optional — deploy to Vercel for a shareable link
tasks.description: Next.js + Vercel, needs env var for session/auth. Only if Loom isn't enough.

name: Fun Building
description: A web-based house builder game for kids. Place rooms, add furniture, paint everything. Built by Elli + kid together, AI writes the code. Phase 1: static 3D builder with localStorage saves. Phase 2: NPCs, time/seasons, avatars, in-game coins.

tasks.task: Phase 1 — Set up project: Vite + Three.js + GitHub Pages
tasks.description: Scaffold the repo. Vite for build, Three.js for 3D rendering, deploy to GitHub Pages. No backend.

tasks.task: Phase 1 — 3D room builder: drag to place and resize rooms
tasks.description: Core mechanic. Click to add rooms, drag to resize, snap to grid. Three.js with raycasting for interaction.

tasks.task: Phase 1 — Furniture library: drag and drop items into rooms
tasks.description: Panel of furniture items. Drag into scene, place on floors. Start with simple box-style 3D models.

tasks.task: Phase 1 — Color picker: paint walls, floors, furniture, and exterior
tasks.description: Click any surface to paint it. Custom color picker (not just presets). Interior + exterior walls separate.

tasks.task: Phase 1 — Save and load: persist project to localStorage
tasks.description: Serialize scene to JSON, save to localStorage. Load on return. Multiple save slots.

tasks.task: Phase 1 — Camera controls: zoom in/out, pan, rotate around the house
tasks.description: Three.js OrbitControls. Mouse scroll to zoom, drag to rotate, right-click to pan. Touch support for mobile too.

tasks.task: Phase 1 — Share via link: encode scene as URL or export JSON file
tasks.description: View-only share link. Either URL-encoded state or download/upload JSON file. No backend needed.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Character gestures and emotes: flips, celebrations, dances
tasks.description: Characters perform full root-motion emotes — the whole body moves through 3D space, not in-place wiggling. Ronaldo flip = character launches, rotates fully in the air, lands. Face follows the body through the entire motion (no floating head). Built with Three.js AnimationMixer + root motion bone translation. Feels like watching it happen in real life, not like Roblox body-only loops. Start with 5-10 emotes, expandable as add-ons or purchasable with coins.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Simple NPC characters walking around
tasks.description: Basic animated characters with simple pathfinding inside the house. Not AI-driven, just scripted routines.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Day/night lighting cycle
tasks.description: Three.js directional light + ambient. Sky color + light intensity shift over in-game time.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Days of week and seasons
tasks.description: In-game calendar. Season changes ambient color + environment. Days shown in UI.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Game clock with pause, play, and speed controls
tasks.description: Player can pause, play, or skip time. Fast clock mode. Controls shown in HUD.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Avatar system: pick and assign characters to houses
tasks.description: Kid can design avatar sets. Players pick from available avatars. Assign to household.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Visual aging: characters change appearance over in-game time
tasks.description: Kids become adults visually. Multiple life stages. Tied to fast game clock.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — In-game coin and token system
tasks.description: Earn coins by playing. Spend on avatars and furniture. No real money yet.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Avatar marketplace: buy avatars with in-game coins
tasks.description: Kid-designed avatars listed in a shop. Players buy with coins. Eventually real money too.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — 2D floor plan view alongside 3D
tasks.description: Toggle between top-down 2D plan and 3D walkthrough. Edits sync both views.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Add-on: Basic needs system (hunger, hygiene, bathroom, health)
tasks.description: Characters have need bars: hunger, hygiene, bathroom, health. Bars drain over time. Player must fulfill them — eat food, shower, use bathroom, take medicine when sick. If ignored, character gets debuffs (stinky, weak, sick). Keeps players engaged and returning.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Add-on: Sickness and medicine system
tasks.description: Characters can get sick (low health + untreated needs). Need medicine items placed in the house (cabinet, pharmacy run). Ties into the needs system.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Add-on: Game modes (Hunger Games, Survival, Normal)
tasks.description: Switchable modes on server creation. Normal: relaxed life sim. Survival: needs drain faster, sickness spreads. Hunger Games: last one standing, competitive. Mode is set per server, not per player.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Add-on: Age-appropriate content system — base game is always kid-safe
tasks.description: Base game has zero violence, no weapons, safe for all ages by default. Content rating visible on every server. Super admin can enforce platform-wide content limits.

tasks.task: Phase 2 — Add-on: Combat and weapons mod (off by default, server owner opt-in)
tasks.description: Optional mod enabled only by server owner at room creation. Adds weapons, combat, GTA-style action — non-realistic, cartoon style, no gore. Not accessible in Normal or kid-safe servers. Admin can disable this mod platform-wide.

tasks.task: Phase 3 — Firebase auth and cloud saves
tasks.description: User accounts. Save projects to cloud. Friends can view each other's builds. Share link becomes persistent.

tasks.task: Phase 3 — Friends can edit shared builds
tasks.description: Multiplayer editing. Real-time or async. Needs Firebase or similar backend.

tasks.task: Phase 3 — Private server rooms: create or join a room with a code
tasks.description: Each player can create a private room and share a join code with friends. Like Minecraft realms or Among Us lobbies. Firebase Realtime DB or websockets. Other players see the same world in real time.

tasks.task: Phase 3 — Single-player vs multiplayer mode toggle
tasks.description: On game start, player picks: play solo or join/create a room. Solo uses localStorage only. Multiplayer connects to the server room.

tasks.task: Phase 3 — Report system: players can flag/report others for bad behavior
tasks.description: Any player can report another. Report goes to admin queue with reason + timestamp. Reports stored in Firebase.

tasks.task: Phase 3 — Admin panel: review reports and ban/block players
tasks.description: Admin sees report queue. Can warn, kick, or permanently ban a player. Banned players cannot join rooms or log in.

tasks.task: Phase 3 — Super admin role: owner can demote or block rogue admins
tasks.description: One super admin account (the game owner). Can remove admin privileges from any admin. Prevents admin abuse. Super admin cannot be blocked by regular admins.

tasks.task: Phase 3 — Self-service server creation: any player can open their own server instantly
tasks.description: No permission needed. Any logged-in player can create a named private server, share a join code, and become that server's owner. Admin and super admin can still shut down the server or ban the owner if they break rules.

name: Private RAG
description: Personal private RAG system over Obsidian vault. Multilingual (Hebrew + English). Covers therapy transcripts, research, kids notes, and general knowledge.

tasks.task: Ingest pipeline — chunk + embed files from Obsidian vault
tasks.description: Support .md, .txt, .pdf. Tag by category (therapy, research, kids, general).

tasks.task: Multilingual embeddings (Hebrew + English)
tasks.description: Use paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 — handles Hebrew natively, no translation needed.

tasks.task: /rag slash command
tasks.description: Query the RAG store from any Hermes session. Returns relevant chunks as context.

tasks.task: Separate ChromaDB collection
tasks.description: Keep isolated from existing agent memory store (chroma_data).
